title: atriplex acanthocarpa subsp. coahuilensis
intro: Atriplex acanthocarpa subsp. coahuilensis, also known as Coahuila thorny saltbush, is a subspecies of shrub in the amaranth family native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which help to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and act as an expectorant to relieve coughs and congestion. Traditional herbal preparations of Atriplex acanthocarpa subsp. coahuilensis include teas, infusions, and salves made from the plant's leaves, stems, and roots, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
